clusters.headline: DR Congo bans mass gatherings in the  capital to prevent spread of Ebola
clusters.summary: Opposition politicians accuse the government of using the outbreak to halt a planned protest.

clusters.headline: South Africa: Ramaphosa Urges Calm Ahead of Anti-Migrant Protests - South African News Briefs - June 30, 2026
Africa: CPJ, Partners Condemn Targeting of Journalists Covering South Africa's Anti-Migrant Protests
clusters.summary: [CPJ] The Committee to Protect Journalists has joined seven other media freedom organizations in condemning attacks on foreign nationals and the targeting of journalists covering anti-migrant protests in South Africa.

clusters.headline: Heavy security in South Africa as anti-migrant protesters take to the streets
clusters.summary: Thousands of people have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

clusters.headline: Pride Month has great significance for those who feel invisible. In Slovenia, many LGBTQ+ people understand just what that means
clusters.summary: One of the first steps taken by Slovenia's new right-wing government in June was to remove the rainbow flag outside the Culture Ministry. As Pride Month draws to a close, DW surveys the situation of the LGBTQ+ community.

clusters.headline: Four men nabbed as Singapore cracks down on first suspected transnational Kpod syndicate under tougher anti-vaping law
clusters.summary: The case follows an earlier S$800,000 Kpod bust, with suppliers and importers now facing significantly harsher jail terms and caning under the amended law.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Top Boko Haram, ISWAP Commanders Surrender As Troops Intensify Operations Against Terrorists in North-East
clusters.summary: [This Day] Abuja -- The Joint Task Force (North East), Operation Hadin Kai (OPHK), has recorded another major breakthrough in its counter-terrorism campaign, with top Boko Haram and Islamic State West Africa Province (ISWAP) commanders abandoning their enclaves and surrendering to troops following s

clusters.headline: Zimbabwe: Zimbabwe Waives Import Duty for South Africa Returnees, Imposes Strict Conditions to Curb Abuse
clusters.summary: [New Zimbabwe] GOVERNMENT has announced a special duty waiver for Zimbabweans returning from South Africa, but has imposed strict conditions aimed at preventing "abuse by non-qualifying individuals".

clusters.headline: Hong Kong prison service pulls controversial AI-generated K-pop ad after anti-drug messaging backfires
clusters.summary: Featuring four female K-pop stars who later transform into old men behind bars, the ad was meant to convey the message that drugs will ruin lives “no matter how pretty they're packaged”.

clusters.headline: 14,000 tickets for Teochew screenings of Dear You sell out in 3 hours, 100 more screenings requested
clusters.summary: Long queues formed at Golden Village cinema outlets as all 40 additional commercial screenings of Dear You in its original Teochew sold out within three hours.

clusters.headline: LTA ramps up efforts against indiscriminate bicycle parking, more spaces planned at transport nodes
clusters.summary: More than 30,000 advisory notices have been issued since 2023 as authorities tackle the persistent issue of bicycles blocking walkways and station entrances.

clusters.headline: Two women to be charged over using S$1.7 million of misappropriated funds on gambling, loans
clusters.summary: One of the women, now aged 65, allegedly misappropriated S$1,729,000 from a bank's vault while working there as a branch manager.
